seed_problem: Frontier-Eng JobShop / ALE-Bench: emit a machine schedule for a JSSP instance; checker validates precedence + no-overlap and scores against the optimum.
mutation: goal+theme: keep the job-shop precedence/no-overlap feasibility structure but replace the makespan objective with TOTAL WEIGHTED COMPLETION TIME (sum_j w_j * C_j, opening time of each weighted ride). The unknown optimum in the score is replaced by a checker-computed, always-feasible fully-serial (ride-index-order) baseline B, so scoring is deterministic (ratio = min(1, 0.1*B/F)). Fresh randomized carnival instances with a difficulty ladder that adds blockbuster (heavy) rides and bottleneck (long) tasks at high testId.
title: Carnival Ride Circuit: Minimum Weighted Ride-Opening Schedule
strategies: serial baseline: run every task back-to-back in ride index order (feasible, weighted opening = B, ratio 0.1)
ECT dispatch: repeatedly schedule the frontier task that finishes earliest at its earliest feasible slot
WSPT / weighted dispatch: prioritize rides with high weight per remaining work so popular rides open first
multi-restart randomized list scheduling: try many static ride priorities and keep the schedule with least weighted opening time
local search / shifting-bottleneck refinement on top of a good dispatch
